hero.title: Nuclear medicine: When the nucleus makes a difference in healthcare
hero.description: Explore careers, knowledge, and scholarship opportunities through a modern visual experience designed for Vietnamese students.

matchmaker.title: AI Career Matchmaker (Mockup)
matchmaker.description: What do you enjoy? Pick your interest to see a career suggestion.

code.title: Suggested: AI Engineer in Healthcare
code.detail: You are a strong fit for building AI models for PET/SPECT image reading, optimizing hospital workflows, and creating clinical decision assistants.

medical.title: Suggested: Nuclear Medicine Physician
medical.detail: You can follow a medical pathway focused on radionuclide-based diagnosis and therapy, combining clinical practice with advanced technology.

machine.title: Suggested: Nuclear Medical Equipment Engineer
machine.detail: You may thrive in designing and operating PET/CT and SPECT/CT systems, as well as quality assurance and radiation safety.

research.title: Suggested: Biomedical and Nuclear Research Scientist
research.detail: You can work on new radiotracers, improve diagnostic workflows, and contribute to breakthroughs in university labs or research institutes.

communication.title: Suggested: Healthcare Science Communication Specialist
communication.detail: You are a good fit for turning complex nuclear medicine concepts into clear, engaging content for students, families, and communities.

curiosity.title: Learn science in an exciting way
curiosity.detail: Physics, Chemistry, and Biology become vivid when you see how they directly connect to real medical images and devices.

impact.title: Create impact early
impact.detail: Even in middle and high school, you can join STEM clubs and small projects that support healthier communities.

future.title: Open many future pathways
future.detail: From this field, you can grow into a doctor, healthcare AI engineer, imaging systems engineer, or research scientist.

timeline.title: Roadmap from HUST to the world
timeline.description: A sample path for students going from technical foundations to international opportunities.

items.desc: Build a strong base in Math, Physics, Programming, and academic English.
Work on AI healthcare projects, medical imaging pipelines, detector simulation, and lab practice.
Apply to programs in Germany/Russia and broaden your research in Medical Physics.
Work in research institutes, high-tech hospitals, or healthtech startups.

game.title: NuMed Games
game.intro: Clear 3 science puzzles to unlock your trainee badge in an 8-bit pixel game vibe.
game.levelHint: Level 2 and 3 are temporarily locked for balanced progression.
game.wrongReveal: Too bad, radiation got you! GAME OVER
game.victoryDesc: You cleared all 3 obstacles and reached the finish flag.
game.gameOverDesc: Too bad, radiation got you! GAME OVER

questions.title: The Glowing Banana Case
questions.prompt: Did you know that eating a banana gives your body a tiny amount of natural radiation? What happens if you eat 100 bananas at once?

options.text: You will glow in the dark.
Nothing scary; the exposure is still very small.
You will break all electronic devices nearby.

questions.explain: Great choice. Natural radiation exists around us all the time. In nuclear medicine, tracer doses are carefully controlled: enough for imaging signals, not for dramatic movie effects.

questions.title: The Doctor’s Magic Lens
questions.prompt: In a nuclear medicine room (SPECT/PET), what is the scanner’s main role?

options.text: It blasts strong rays into the body to burn unhealthy cells immediately.
It captures bones exactly like a regular X-ray.
It records signals emitted from the tracer inside the body.
It measures skin temperature to predict disease.

questions.explain: Surprise. Unlike X-ray imaging, the signal source here is inside the body. PET/SPECT acts like a functional lens, revealing how tissues and organs are working.

questions.title: Temporary Superpower
questions.prompt: After receiving a tracer injection for nuclear medicine imaging, which temporary superpower does your body gain?

options.text: Emitting tiny signals that scanners can detect to build functional images.
Turning your body into a metal magnet.
Becoming completely immune to all diseases.

questions.explain: Exactly. For a short time, your body becomes a tiny lighthouse emitting measurable signals. That data helps clinicians evaluate internal biological activity.

wiki.title: Knowledge Wiki
wiki.description: A foundational library on isotopes, diagnostic techniques, and clinical applications.

items.useCase: PET/CT for oncology and neurology diagnostics
items.note: Widely used in FDG-PET to observe glucose metabolism.

items.useCase: SPECT for cardiology, bone, and renal imaging
items.note: A common isotope due to its favorable diagnostic properties.
Supports both diagnosis and targeted radionuclide therapy.

career.title: Community & Career Guidance
career.description: A space to share learning experiences, skill roadmaps, and internship opportunities.

community.title: Humans of Nuclear Medicine
community.subtitle: Discover stories from people who apply nuclear technologies every day to help save lives.

personas.name: Dr. Linh Tran
personas.quote: We do not just read images - we read each patient's chance to survive.
personas.story: I once thought I would follow a conventional clinical path. But the first time I saw a PET scan image from a cancer patient, I realized there are things the naked eye cannot detect while technology can reveal them clearly. My work is to interpret those signals so diseases can be detected earlier and more accurately.

personas.name: Nguyen Hoang
personas.quote: Science is not distant - it is gradually becoming someone else's lifeline.
personas.story: I do not work directly with patients. But every day in the lab, I know what I research can impact thousands of lives. Our team is developing new radiotracers that deliver clearer images, lower doses, and better safety.

personas.name: Le Tuan
personas.quote: A precise system enables a precise diagnosis - and that can save lives.
personas.story: People often think doctors are the only key factor. But behind every diagnosis is a full technical system that must operate accurately down to milliseconds. I am responsible for keeping PET/CT systems stable and reliable. Even a slight deviation can affect results.

personas.name: Mai Anh
personas.quote: You do not need to be great from day one - you only need to stay curious long enough.
personas.story: I used to be afraid of physics. But in university, I learned how physics is applied in medicine. That is when I realized these are not meaningless numbers - they are tools to understand diseases and help heal people.

universitiesPage.title: University & Scholarship Guidance
universitiesPage.subtitle: Explore universities with strong tracks in Nuclear Medicine, Healthcare AI, and Biomedical Engineering to design your best-fit pathway.

universities.name: Tomsk Polytechnic University (TPU)
universities.fit: Students aiming for international research in nuclear technologies for diagnosis and therapy.
universities.scholarship: Russian Government scholarships and project-based research funding.

universities.name: NRNU MEPhI
universities.fit: Students who want deep expertise in nuclear physics, detectors, and radiation technology in medicine.

universities.name: Hanoi University of Science and Technology (HUST)
universities.major: Biomedical Engineering, Medical Electronics, AI in Healthcare
universities.fit: Learners who want a strong engineering base and AI-driven clinical technology applications.
universities.scholarship: Merit scholarships, student research grants, and industry-funded awards.

universities.name: Ho Chi Minh City University of Technology (HCMUT)
universities.fit: Students who want to combine engineering, coding, and data for real clinical problems.

universities.name: University of Science - VNUHCM (HCMUS)

universities.name: University of Science - VNU Hanoi (VNU-HUS)

universities.scholarship: Merit scholarships, hospital internship support, and medical partner sponsorships.

journey.videoPlaceholder: Video Placeholder (YouTube embed will be added here)

islands.title: Birth Island
islands.content: Welcome to Island 1 – The Birth Island! This is the energy supply station for our whole journey. Here, we will meet the giant Papa Moly-99s. Even though they look quiet and just like to sit still to decay slowly, each Papa Moly is actually holding a super active little hero inside! Inside the island's lab, a silent magical change happens every second. When Papa Moly decides to give away some of his energy through decay, a little superhero is born. That is Technetium-99m! Unlike his calm dad, superhero Tc-99m has a health bar (half-life) that drops super fast – in just 6 hours! This little guy is always in an excited state, ready to shoot out bright Gamma rays to help doctors light up all the mysteries inside the body. Because this superhero is so active and quick, we must take him to the next bus stop right away before his energy runs out!

islands.title: Transfer Station
islands.content: The 0.9% saline water acts like a shuttle bus running through the column of atoms to create a magical separation between Papa Moly and Tc-99m. While Papa Moly holds tightly to his spot inside the machine, the active Tc-99m superheroes quickly dissolve into the water stream to get out. The result of this milking process is a clear bottle of liquid holding billions of light detectives, ready to leave their papa's arms and set off on a journey to save people!

islands.title: Friendship Island
islands.content: Welcome to Friendship Island, where miracles that save patients begin! Here, even though superhero detective Tc-99m has a bright gamma flashlight to see through the body, he gets lost very easily and wanders around like a tourist without a map. To keep detective Tc-99m from getting lost, we need to attach him to a smart guide Robot with a super GPS brain (a carrier) to make a perfect team called a Radiopharmaceutical. Thanks to these helpful Robots—like the MDP Robot who is an expert at finding tiny bone cracks, infections, or bone cancer—detective Tc-99m will be guided by the hand straight to the exact target address in the body with perfect accuracy!

islands.title: Observation Deck
islands.content: This is the final and most important stop of our journey! Our glowing detective, the radiopharmaceutical Tc-99m, now enters the patient's body. It follows the bloodstream to find the exact target organ and stays right there. Here, it turns on its gamma flashlight to report the situation back to base. The island's secret: A giant SPECT scanner will catch these glowing signals and build a vivid 3D map of the inside organs. This helps doctors find out exactly what is wrong and heal the patient perfectly without any surgery!

radiopharmaceutical.intro: In the simplest terms, a radiopharmaceutical is a special type of drug that emits invisible radiation. Unlike conventional medicines, it is used to map organ activity or precisely destroy diseased cells.
radiopharmaceutical.partsTitle: In essence, a radiopharmaceutical is a combination of two inseparable components:
parts: Part 1 - Transport vehicle (biological carrier): a chemical compound or biological molecule that automatically targets specific organs or cell types (thyroid, bones, heart, cancer cells).

Part 2 - Signal source (radioisotope): firmly attached to the carrier; once it reaches the destination, it emits radiation that PET/SPECT can capture for diagnosis, or uses that energy for tumor destruction from within.
radiopharmaceutical.conclusion: This combination allows doctors to detect disease very early at the cellular level, before clear structural changes appear.

quests.title: Today's Quests
quests.loginRequired: Please login to save your EXP!

tasks.title: Read 1 Wiki article on Tc-99m Isotope
Ask the AI Mentor any medical question

ai.title: Math & Programming Foundations
details: Master Python, C++, and scientific computing libraries (NumPy, SciPy).
Build a strong foundation in Linear Algebra and Probability & Statistics to understand model behavior deeply.
Develop solid skills in data structures, optimization algorithms, and engineering tools (Git, Docker).

ai.title: Medical Image Processing
details: Process medical imaging data in DICOM and NIfTI formats from SPECT/CT systems.
Train segmentation and object detection models (U-Net family, YOLO) for lesion and tumor localization.
Optimize hardware utilization and accelerate inference on specialized GPU infrastructure.

ai.title: Digital Twin & RAG Agent
details: Build RAG agent pipelines that retrieve medical literature and combine it with patient records.
Fine-tune large language models (LLMs) for automated clinical report generation.
Design Digital Twin simulation architectures to test personalized radiotherapy strategies.

ai.title: AI Engineer at Hospital/Startup
details: Develop AI-powered clinical decision support systems (CDSS) to reduce physician workload.
Deploy end-to-end machine learning systems in hospital settings with reliable workflow integration.
Contribute to core technology research and build breakthrough HealthTech solutions.

radiation.title: Radiation Physics & Radiochemistry
details: Master atomic structure, nuclear structure, and radioactive decay chains.
Understand how ionizing radiation interacts with matter and biological tissues.
Practice core radiochemistry lab techniques and foundational radiation safety principles.

radiation.title: Radiopharmaceutical Preparation
details: Operate Generator systems (such as Mo-99/Tc-99m) to elute medical radioisotopes.
Perform radiolabeling workflows that bind radioisotopes to carrier compounds (kits).
Run radiopharmaceutical quality control (QC): radiochemical purity, biological quality, and physical parameters.

radiation.title: Radiation Measurement & Safety
details: Use specialized radiation measurement instruments (ionization chamber, scintillation counters).
Evaluate dose exposure and apply ALARA radiation protection principles rigorously.
Manage, classify, and dispose of medical radioactive waste according to international standards.

radiation.title: Hospital Radiopharmacist
details: Collaborate with physicians to design optimal radiopharmaceutical protocols for diagnosis and therapy (Theranostics).
Lead comprehensive QA/QC operations for nuclear medicine laboratory workflows in hospitals.
Participate in R&D of next-generation radiopharmaceuticals for earlier and more accurate disease detection.
